rejectedPatterns.note: owner review 002 rejected the language-first model; listed here only as rejected

rules: country segment = lowercase country slug (kz, pl, de)
language segment = lowercase ISO 639-1 code; Kazakh = kk never kz (/kz/kz/ forbidden); Ukrainian = uk never ua
stable Latin content slugs identical across variants; never translated/transliterated
lowercase-hyphen; trailing-slash canonical; no query-parameter content routes
routes exist only for matrix-supported market-language pairs (no Cartesian product)
new market routes owner-gated before creation

coreDataModel.languageCode: ru | kk | en | pl | uk | de (ISO 639-1; kk never kz; uk never ua)
coreDataModel.localeTag: ru-KZ | kk-KZ | en-KZ | pl-PL | uk-PL | ru-PL | en-PL | de-DE | en-DE (BCP 47)
coreDataModel.urlPrefix: kz/ru | kz/kk | kz/en (derived, never stored as one authoritative locale variable)
rules: country and language separate fields end-to-end
country selects factual MI; language selects presentation only

contract: global pages = platform-wide/global editorial context; no local-eligibility claims
market pages are first-class indexable pages once publication-authorized
market pages never canonicalized to global pages
global routes never silently IP-redirected to a country (dismissible suggestions only)
legacy routes remain until a separately authorized production-routing migration

duplicateIntentResolutions.marketRoot: /kz/ru/ (and siblings) is the canonical Kazakhstan market homepage AND country market root — one surface; /kz/ru/countries/kazakhstan/ is prohibited (never created; incoming shape 301s to /kz/ru/; never indexed); global /countries/ stays a market directory
duplicateIntentResolutions.exchangeInMarket: /kz/{lang}/exchanges/{exchange}/ is the single canonical page for the '{exchange} in {country}' intent (pair facts inherent); no competing deep-passport page; optional /kz/{lang}/exchanges/{exchange}/availability/ evidence subpage only with distinct documentary purpose, owner-gated
duplicateIntentResolutions.methodology: global /methodology/ = platform-wide trust page; /kz/{lang}/methodology/ per LIVE language with market-specific verification context; distinct intents, cross-linked, no duplicated copy

canonicalHreflang.xDefaultRule: market clusters: x-default → the market's LIVE marketDefault sibling (KZ → /kz/ru/, PL → /pl/pl/, DE → /de/de/) per LOCALE22/LOCALE23; English never assumed default or x-default (LOCALE24); global clusters: x-default → global English URL
rules: LIVE = the specific page's PageLocaleCoverage record is LIVE (market support alone adds nothing — LOCALE16/LOCALE18); emit only for LIVE siblings
regional BCP 47 tags mandatory for market pages (ru-KZ/kk-KZ/en-KZ); bare ru/kk/en forbidden there
clusters generated from one routing manifest (reciprocity cannot drift)
market pages join clusters only after publication GO
regional tags mandatory: ru-KZ/kk-KZ/en-KZ/pl-PL/uk-PL/ru-PL/en-PL/de-DE/en-DE; bare codes forbidden on market pages

polandCluster.rule: never English while the Polish market-default variant is LIVE

localizedMetadataSchema: translated title/description with per-script length lint
localized OG text over the market's single country artwork
schema in page language (inLanguage = localeTag) with stable entity IDs
MI facts identical across language siblings; structured data never forks facts by language

sitemapRobots.sitemap: explicit allowlist; LIVE market-language pairs + authorized global routes only; /go/**, /__design/**, previews, prohibited duplicates excluded; optional per-market partitions

seoGates.SEO05: sitemap allowlist = published LIVE routes only
seoGates.SEO06: schema valid, mirrors content, inLanguage correct
seoGates.SEO08: internal links resolve and stay in current market-language namespace
seoGates.SEO10: slug uniqueness; no duplicate-intent routes (market-root + exchange-in-market prohibitions)
seoGates.SEO11: CWV budget; LCP = first-screen commercial surface; zero CLS from fixed tiles/mastheads
seoGates.SEO13: hreflang complete, reciprocal, regional-tagged, x-default per rule
seoGates.SEO14: no IP/browser hard redirects or cloaking
seoGates.SEO15: no fallback page indexed as translated

localeGatesRef: LOCALE01–LOCALE25 defined in CBW_CI_QA_STATUS_AND_OWNER_GATE_MATRIX_v1 and enforced together with SEO gates

lifecycle.note: implementation and production remain separately unauthorized
